reviews.review_description: Yes, Mare is light on storyline and uses only the simplest of controller features, however, I enjoyed it immensely. The landscapes and scenery are visually stunning; the characters are pleasant; and the unfolding of the journey is engaging. Ideal for those of us new to VR looking for relaxation, escape, and adventure.

I would say it's more like experience than game but it is atmospheric and very laid back the art style is lovely the music is very nice the gameplay it's very minimum and story is head scratching but in kind good way I would recommend it when it's cheap

reviews.review_title: Can't believe I wasted my money on this.
reviews.review_description: Bad enough you have to electrocute a kid to get her loose from chains, but, it didn't get any better from there. The video affects were excellent but the game play is horrible.

I've played like 3 parts and didn't know what I was doing, after a couple of years I got back to it from the beginning and got the hang of it. Once I understood what I had to do, and it's pretty simple besides a few bunnies which were hard to get, then it was fun, relaxing, kind of trip of some sort. It's true it's not for everyone but I enjoyed. I wish the story made more sense though.

breathtaking sprawling landscapes uncovering mystery ruins call for you as you follow a little girl crash landed from intense storm guided by a thunderbird into the mechanical heart of the land 

you guide the bird from pole to pole, girl follows him through different paths. Choose wisely and you may see real ending, open to interpretation like any good poem 

this is high up there with Ico and Rime in my book

reviews.review_title: Believe the Low Reviews On This One...
reviews.review_description:
This game is one that caught my eye well over a year ago due to it's overall design and concept, but the overtly negative reviews and general lack of real gameplay or story kept me away.
So it now being on sale for a dirt cheap price made me feel more safe trying it out for myself... and everything I read and my original ideas I had after reading, seeing, and hearing things about the game were utterly on point sadly.
The game looks great for the most part, and I love the ambiance of what little I experienced, but the gameplay is 99% nonexistent. This in itself wouldn't at all be an issue for me if there was any kind of actual story being told (which environmental 100% interpretiv "storytelling" doesn't count for me). So you are left with pointing, clicking, and waiting all for nothing but seeing an overly similar landscape in the next area. Rinse and repeat with zero real narrative reward or any drive to keep going. Even if I forced myself to keep going, I definitely would never do it again, making this a massive waste of money. ESPECIALLY at full price. The developers REALLY should have told a cohesive story (didn't evenhave to be deep or complex, just anything wouldhave helped) with a lively character or three at least to carry this "game" as it is currently. Sadly, that's not the case...
So I am getting a refund to get the few dollars I spent on it back that could be spent MUCH better elsewhere.

Mare takes you on an adventure to get from point A to point B in a barren and seemingly broken world (war-time / post war / apocalyptic). There are occasionally some challenges to determining and following the path, but by and large you must just follow a  prescribed path.

Parents beware: In my opinion this adventure should be rated Adults Only, as your way of getting around is by electrocuting everything in site. On several occasions you are required to electrocute a young girl, other creatures or strange beings (on other occasions it brings the girl back to life), and you cannot proceed unless you choose to make these decisions. These are not choices a child should be confronted with.

I almost stopped pursuing this adventure as I found this aspect horrible, but decided to put my feelings aside to see it to conclusion.  Perhaps the game creator’s objective is to leave you with a sense of despair (?)

The game mechanics are good and the scenery intriguing, but otherwise there is little point to it other than getting through by following the prescribed path and collecting things on the way.

Warning: Spoiler alert (but also reasons not to buy!)

So I'm probably spoiled now that I played Moss I and II... the graphics and the music were so incredible.  But this game sounded interesting and was on sale, so I decided to try it.  Uhhg...  I am seriously gonna try to get my money back.

First, the graphics are really quite awful.  Every turn requires a black out and screen reload... so it's not a true VR world, it's just multiple 3D scenes you can switch between.   And the whole game is point and click to move this bird from one spot to another.  (Note: You are not the bird, you control the bird.  I thought this might give me a sense of flying.  Nope.  You're just high up most of the time.)  

And then the story line seems really umm... twisted.  You find a young girl - what, 8? 10? And she's CHAINED to a container.  Who chains a little girl in a box????  You have to electrocute her to free her.  What???  And everything is about electricity in a war destroyed maze city.  The city makes absolutely no sense (like even before it was destroyed by bombshells).  But as soon as you free the girl, you just point and click to difference places and then sit there... and sit there... while she goes through the maze and catches up to you and opens a door to give you a new place to fly to, so you can sit and wait for her to catch up again.  THATS LIKE-- the whole game!!  Trying to get her to follow you (or you follow her) so you can find new "electrical" points you can fly to.  Oh, and then she kinda "dies"... and you pretty much have to let her just to get through a door... and becomes like a ghost.  Which is very sick and twisted of a game to do.  Again, she's this cute little girl.  Doesn't speak English or anything... but she is the ONLY real color of the whole game.  (I'm pretty sure the rest of the game has less than 10 colors all in washed out yellows and grays.)  And then cuz it's a weird game, she comes back.  But it's still just point, click, fly, and wait.  And your trigger buttons are the only thing that do anything... all other buttons open the menu.  Pretty sure this was created by beginner VR designers and coders.  Oh, and music?  Nothing but a surreal, probably computer generated, background "ambiance".  Not real music or anything that adds to the game play. 

I'm sorry.  I was not only extremely bored, but those drawn to the game might be because of the bird, which means possible nature lovers and there's little to no nature in this, save for some horses you have to throw lightening at to get them to move, or maybe VR lovers, and it's not a true VR environment, or people who like puzzles, and these are fairly mundane and stupid, and all those types of people will be extremely disappointed if they're looking for a beautiful game, with interesting game play.

Don't waste your money.  If you love nature, or beauty, music, or really interesting puzzles and story lines that promote good (not show and promote serious messed up evil to a little kids)... go buy Moss instead.

Or at least if you enjoyed Moss, you WONT likely enjoy this!

reviews.review_title: How is this getting such good reviews!
reviews.review_description:
who did they pay for these reviews here and elsewhere online? The game is beautiful I will give you that but it ends there. The gameplay is terrible. A nonexistent story line has you solving puzzles that mostly just point and click to the next post...not really a puzzle...so easy I was bored and wondered how well my 4-year-old niece would do with it. 
I will say the last two chapters of the game had puzzles that were a tiny bit more difficult and more like what I was expecting.  But overall there was no discernable story other than "You are in a nightmare land and are trying to go somewhere unknown in it" the end was even more confusing than the rest I have no idea if I messed up and lost or if that was the intended end of the story? In fact I literally googled if that's how the game is supposed to end.
Felt more like a how-to VR tutorial or like I was learning how to play a game that never started.
Definitely would not purchase or play again. 
Also zero explanation of how the game works I picked up bonus "crystal cats" but don't know why, if I got them all do they unlock something? or just pointless? also not going back to find out, whatever they unlock I'm sure it isn't worth it unless it's an explanation of the storyline.
And what was up with that dragon?! LOL RANDOM

reviews.review_title: A must have vr game and experience
reviews.review_description:
Beautiful game with interesting puzzles set in majestic environments accompanied by a beautiful soundtrack.

This is the game I will show my friends when they want to try vr. Because it's fun, charming, without gimmicks, and visually stunning in vr.

If Disney let David Lynch remake Monsters Inc when he was in a particularly abstract mood, then we’d probably get something like this. Mare is replete with a dreamlike quality that shows us a world governed by an infernal machine, powered by dark electricity generated by a young girl’s screams as she is led through an Escher inspired landscape by a clockwork owl that may not be what it seems. 

First of all, the game design is weakest aspect of Mare. While there is a slight puzzle element to the experience, it only tentatively approaches that genre on a handful of occasions. You’re unlikely to get stumped at any point; instead, the frustration is likely to come from a realisation of the potential this had to actually be quite an ingenious puzzler. Essentially, it’s a point and click sightseeing tour that’s far more likely to occupy your mind with WTF is going on than any cognitive tasks set by the game.

Fortunately, this esoteric approach wins through thanks to beautiful presentation and a deliberate refusal to give us any direct context or verbose clarification. It does owe a significant debt to Ico and The Last Guardian, and though it’s nowhere as compelling on an interactive level, it successfully evokes the same atmosphere as Team Ico’s output. 

Perhaps it is a wasted opportunity for what could have been just as an involving game as its aesthetic, but it’s far from a waste of time as an experience. £15 is a bit steep for it, mind!
I've had this game for 6 months and I still don't know what I'm doing, help?!

developer_response.body: Hi, feel free to send us an email to contact@visiontrick.com and we'll help you out!

reviews.review_description: Absolutely gorgeous really enjoyed it only wish there were more like this on quest I would have paid top dollar for this worth every penny and not a zombie insight

Come in expecting a beautiful, mesmerising, logic based experience and you won't be disappointed.

If you like any Team Ico games, this is a MUST buy!

(Would be awesome to see through the birds eyes while it flies or play as the girl).

This is what VR is all about, immersive experiences. 

I LOVE IT!

Had potential but I don't think it quite lives up to it though, puzzles are few and far between and aren't really very difficult, more along lines of move this over here and then press that and then the next door will open, but not imaginative. It's a nice enough looking game and might be what you are looking for, whilst I did find it ok to play, the more I've thought about it the more dissatisfied I've actually been.

I think there's a lot about this game that will unfortunately welcome reductive and dismissive criticisms at face value. For one it's quite short and quite passive. secondly, it wears its inspirations in its sleeve.

But that doesn't mean it doesn't have some tricks of its own. It's beautiful, lovingly crafted, really intriguing, and well paced. I completedit in an evening and it felt precisely as long as it ought to be, but I'm certain it'll be I  my mind for quite a bit longer.

Certainly a brave move, making a game that so welcomes comparisons to some of the absolute best games ever made. Happy to report it doesn't entirely live in Ueda's shadow. It's a wonderful experience on its own merits. And while it's passive, there are some key moments of agency that are really impactful.

Thanks for making this one, folks. I'm excited to see where you go next. <3

Let me start with some positive... The game looks sort of good... Now, with that out of the way...

This game is insanely boring! Point and click, and then wait. There is NO story! I mean, there could be one but it is way to cryptic for me to dig out. There is NO explanation of why you do what you do, or why the things that happen happens.

This game is only for pseudo intellectuals.

No... Just NO!
Univers le plus grandiose mysterieux et contemplatif que j'ai pu voir en VR ! Gameplay très basique mais les énigmes sont très bien amenées. Le jeux rappelle un peu the last Giardian. Bravo !

I love this video game. The smooth and calm music was fantastic fit 4 it. The graphics are gorgeous. And the store and puzzles are fantastic. I am only on level 5 but once I beat it I will update my review. But 4 now I am so glad that I bought this. The developers did a superb job on this video game.
amazing!!!!! I recommend you this experience, don’t research about this game just play it
